title: Contracts for Difference (CfD) Allocation Round 7: standard terms and conditions
body: " 31 July 2025: All documents updated  We have amended the contract documents for AR7/AR7a to ensure that the contract terms reflect government policy as set out in recent government responses to AR7 policy consultations published on GOV.UK.  The main amendments are:   in all documents where required, the provisions in respect of the contract Term have been corrected to allow for 20 year contracts to be allocated to all wind technologies and solar PV in all documents where required, the Base Year has been amended to 2024 in all documents, the Milestone Requirement that requires that all Relevant Applicable Planning Consents are secured by the Milestone Delivery Date (Technology-Specific Project Commitments) are amended to clarify that this requirement applies to Unconsented Projects only in the 6 phased offshore wind projects  the definition of “Longstop Period” has been amended to instruct the CfD Counterparty to insert the correct Longstop Period as specified in Table H of the CfD Standard Terms Notice provision is added to enable the CfD Counterparty to insert whether or not a project is an Unconsented Project   in phase 1 of the Apportioned and Single phased metering contracts, the provisions relating to the “Milestone Requirement Notice” have been amended to ensure that requirement applies to both the Project Commitments and 10% spend route when evidencing fulfilment of a Milestone Requirement    These are the final versions of the CfD Standard Terms and Conditions, the Generic Agreement and contract variants for CfD Allocation Round 7.  Each CfD contract issued in Allocation Round 7 (AR7) will comprise 2 components:   the specific front-end CfD Agreement the Standard Terms and Conditions   The generic CfD Agreement forms the front section of the CfD contract and contains project-specific information that tailors the application of the Standard Terms and Conditions. CfD applicants should select this CfD Agreement, unless one of the other published variations is required - for example the phased (apportioned and single metering) terms or private network terms.  "
